title: Organizer playbook (planning, roles, layout, PII-free intake/triage flow, safety-rules one-pager, wrap-up, local-resource pointers)
context: The organizer playbook is the primary value artifact for first-time community repair event organizers. It must give end-to-end coverage: planning timeline, venue checklist, roles (host/greeter/triage/fixer/safety lead), station layout, a PII-free item-intake/triage ticket template, volunteer onboarding, a safety-rules one-pager, post-event wrap-up, and pointers (not advice) to local insurance/liability and waste-stream resources. The intake ticket must collect item type + fault + outcome only — no attendee personal data — and must carry a visible 'do not record personal data' notice. Liability/insurance is handled as pointers to local guidance only; no on-platform form ingests personal data.
objective: Author the complete organizer playbook covering all required sections, ensure the intake/triage ticket template collects no PII, verify all factual claims are source-cited and license-clean, and have it reviewed by an approver distinct from the author.
acceptanceCriteria: End-to-end coverage so a first-time organizer can plan, staff, and safely run a community repair event; sections include planning timeline, venue checklist, roles, station layout, volunteer onboarding, safety-rules one-pager, post-event wrap-up, and local-resource pointers

The intake/triage ticket template collects item type, fault description, and outcome only — no attendee name, contact details, or other personal data; a visible 'do not record personal data' notice is present on the template
Liability and insurance are addressed solely as pointers to local guidance sources, not as advice; no on-platform form collects personal data
Every factual claim is traceable to a cited, reuse-permitted source; no verbatim copying of copyrighted or NC-licensed text; no third-party logos; no 'Repair Café' name
The playbook is reviewed by an approver distinct from the author (approvedBy != reviewedBy); a PR-tied review-log entry records both parties, sources checked, and license decisions
Content is published under CC-BY-4.0; renders from the data-002 schema; passes the CI content-governance gate
output: A committed, schema-valid organizer playbook (CC-BY-4.0) with all required sections, a PII-free intake ticket template, source-cited claims, and a PR-tied review-log entry showing distinct author and approver.
